channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
item.description: Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.
item.content:encoded:
Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Shelling at night, gunfire by day in Israel's expanding zone of control in Gaza
item.description: When the U.S. brokered a ceasefire last year, Israel controlled half of Gaza. Now Israeli forces have pushed deeper, and Palestinians are paying a deadly price.
item.content:encoded:
When the U.S. brokered a ceasefire last year, Israel controlled half of Gaza. Now Israeli forces have pushed deeper, and Palestinians are paying a deadly price.

(Image credit: Anas Baba)
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: Trump leans on 'communist' messaging as economic angst drives Democrats
item.description: During the last election, when struggling to find a memorable attack line against Kamala Harris, Trump eventually landed on "Comrade Kamala."
item.content:encoded:
During the last election, when struggling to find a memorable attack line against Kamala Harris, Trump eventually landed on "Comrade Kamala."

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Count Binface: The intergalactic warrior who could upend Britain's strangest election
item.description: Meet Count Binface: the challenger from another planet taking on Nigel Farage as questions over the Reform UK leader's finances overshadow his election comeback.
item.content:encoded:
Meet Count Binface: the challenger from another planet taking on Nigel Farage as questions over the Reform UK leader's finances overshadow his election comeback.

(Image credit: Scott A Garfitt)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: No internet, no screen time? FCC weighs cutting subsidy that lowers school internet bills
item.description: Many schools rely on consumer fees funneled through the federal government to cut internet costs. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr called for ending this program before Donald Trump tapped him for the job.
item.content:encoded:
Many schools rely on consumer fees funneled through the federal government to cut internet costs. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr called for ending this program before Donald Trump tapped him for the job.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: One U.S. visa program is growing rapidly. No one is happy with it.
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are starting to talk about one facet of immigration reform: how to expand the popular H-2A visa program for farm laborers. They face obstacles.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans on Capitol Hill are starting to talk about one facet of immigration reform: how to expand the popular H-2A visa program for farm laborers. They face obstacles.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: One of Spain's deadliest wildfires has killed at least 11 people
item.description: A wildfire in southern Spain has killed at least 11 people, making it one of the country's deadliest on record, as soaring temperatures grip much of the country.
item.content:encoded:
A wildfire in southern Spain has killed at least 11 people, making it one of the country's deadliest on record, as soaring temperatures grip much of the country.

(Image credit: INFOCA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Fighting between U.S. and Iran appears to have stopped as Iran buried its Supreme Leader, TPS holders close to losing work permits, investigation continues into Mississippi teen's death.
item.content:encoded: Fighting between U.S. and Iran appears to have stopped as Iran buried its Supreme Leader, TPS holders close to losing work permits, investigation continues into Mississippi teen's death.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
item.description: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.
item.content:encoded:
New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange intensifying fire across Mideast, threatening ceasefire deal
item.description: Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.
item.content:encoded:
Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.

(Image credit: Hasan Salavati)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
